chunks.text: Alexandra von Meier is a retired Adjunct Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at UC Berkeley. She taught courses on Electric Power Systems and served as Director in CIEE's Electric Grid program area. Her work focused on power distribution systems, Smart Grid issues, and integrating distributed and intermittent generation. Her research utilized high-precision micro-synchrophasor measurements for situational awareness, diagnostics and control in distribution grids.

Alexandra von Meier earned her PhD in Energy and Resources from UC Berkeley in 1995, following an MA in the same field in 1990. She completed her BA in Physics at UC Berkeley in 1986. Her research areas include Power and Energy, specifically Electric Grids and Power Distribution. She is affiliated with the Berkeley Power and Energy Center (BPEC).

Selected publications by Alexandra von Meier include work on harmonic synchrophasor estimation, grid data analytics, and streaming clustering for electric grid events. She has contributed to research on phasor-based control for integrating variable energy resources and precision micro-synchrophasors for distribution systems. Her publications also cover smart grids for renewable energy transition and challenges in renewable resource integration.

Additional publications include a book on electric power systems, studies on occupational cultures affecting technological innovation, and cross-cultural perspectives on nuclear power operations. Her work spans technical, social, and policy aspects of energy systems, reflecting a multidisciplinary approach to power and energy research.
